settings.text: Petalry started as a market stall at the Asheville City Market. What began as weekend bunches has grown into a full-service flower studio — but the philosophy hasn’t changed. We work seasonally, source locally when we can, and believe the best arrangements are the ones that look like they were just picked from a garden.

settings.name: Lily Marchetti
settings.bio: Former landscape designer turned florist. Lily started Petalry at the Asheville City Market in 2019 and hasn’t looked back. She leads all wedding and event design.

settings.name: Rosa Delgado
settings.bio: Rosa keeps the studio running — sourcing, scheduling, deliveries, and workshops. She also teaches most of our beginner classes.

settings.title: Locally Grown
settings.description: We partner with farms in Western North Carolina for seasonal blooms and foliage whenever possible.

settings.title: Seasonal First
settings.description: Our arrangements change with the seasons. What’s in bloom dictates what we design — not the other way around.

settings.title: Sustainable Practices
settings.description: Minimal floral foam, compostable wrapping, and we donate unsold stems to local shelters weekly.

settings.question: Do you grow your own flowers?
settings.answer: We don’t have our own farm, but we work closely with several small farms in the Asheville area. In peak season, most of our stems come from within 50 miles of the studio.

settings.question: Where do you source from in winter?
settings.answer: During the colder months we source from wholesale markets in California and South America, always prioritizing sustainable growers. We also work with more dried and preserved materials in winter.

settings.question: Can I visit the studio?
settings.answer: Yes! Our shop is open Tuesday through Saturday. You’re welcome to walk in, browse, and pick up a fresh bouquet. We love meeting our customers in person.

settings.question: Do you do weddings outside Asheville?
settings.answer: We serve weddings across Western North Carolina, including Brevard, Hendersonville, Black Mountain, and the Blue Ridge Parkway area. Travel fees may apply for venues beyond 30 miles.
